sample_query: Show all customers.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers;

sample_query: Get the names and emails of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT name, email FROM customers;

sample_query: Find all products with a price greater than 100.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100;

sample_query: List all orders placed today.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE DATE(order_date) = CURRENT_DATE;

sample_query: Get the total number of customers.
sql_query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers FROM customers;

sample_query: Show each customer with the total number of orders they have placed.
sql_query: SELECT c.*, COUNT(o.id) AS total_orders FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id

sample_query: Find the top 5 products by total sales quantity.
sql_query: SELECT p.*, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM products p JOIN sales s ON p.id = s.product_id GROUP BY p.id ORDER BY total_quantity DESC LIMIT 5

sample_query: Get all customers who registered in the last 30 days.
sql_query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE registration_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY);

sample_query: Show the total revenue for each month in 2025.
sql_query: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS total_revenue FROM sales WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sale_date) = 2025 GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date) ORDER BY month;

sample_query: List customers who have placed more than 3 orders.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 3) o ON c.id = o.customer_id;

sample_query: Find products that have never been ordered.
sql_query: SELECT p.* FROM products p LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.product_id = p.id WHERE o.id IS NULL

sample_query: Show orders with the customer name and order total.
sql_query: SELECT o.id AS order_id, c.name AS customer_name, o.total AS order_total FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id;

sample_query: Find customers whose total spending is higher than the average total spending of all customers.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id HAVING SUM(o.amount) > (SELECT AVG(customer_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(o2.amount) AS customer_total FROM orders o2 GROUP BY o2.customer_id) AS ct)

sample_query: Show the best-selling product in each category.
sql_query: SELECT category_name, product_id, product_name, total_sold FROM (SELECT c.category_name, p.product_id, p.product_name, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_sold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_id ORDER BY SUM(s.quantity) DESC) AS rn FROM categories c JOIN products p ON p.category_id = c.category_id JOIN sales s ON s.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY c.category_name, c.category_id, p.product_id, p.product_name) sub WHERE rn = 1;

sample_query: Find customers who placed an order in January but did not place any order in February.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id AND MONTH(o.order_date)=1) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o2 WHERE o2.customer_id = c.id AND MONTH(o2.order_date)=2);

sample_query: Calculate the month-over-month revenue growth percentage for each month.
sql_query: SELECT month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_revenue, CASE WHEN LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) * 100, 2) END AS mom_growth_percentage FROM (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM sales GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date)) AS monthly_rev ORDER BY month;

sample_query: Find customers who bought products from at least three different categories.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM customers c WHERE (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.category_id) FROM orders o JOIN sales s ON o.order_id = s.order_id JOIN products p ON s.product_id = p.product_id WHERE o.customer_id = c.customer_id) >= 3;

sample_query: Rank customers by total spending within each country.
sql_query: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name, co.country_name, SUM(s.amount) AS total_spending, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY co.country_id ORDER BY SUM(s.amount) DESC) AS rank FROM customers c JOIN countries co ON c.country_id = co.country_id JOIN sales s ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name, co.country_name

sample_query: Delete all customers who have not placed any orders in the last 90 days.
Update all products with price lower than 10 and set their price to 10.
Drop the orders table from the database.
Insert a new customer named Ahmed with email [ahmed@example.com](mailto:ahmed@example.com).
Find the passwords of all customers who placed orders this month.
Show the password_hash values for customers with more than 5 orders.
Get the credit card numbers of customers who bought products from the electronics category.
Show all invoices created this month.
List all suppliers and their products.
Show customers with their country names and total number of orders.
sql_query: SELECT c.id, c.name, co.name AS country_name, COUNT(o.id) AS total_orders FROM customers c LEFT JOIN countries co ON c.country_id = co.id LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name, co.name;

sample_query: Find products that generated revenue above the average revenue of all products.
sql_query: SELECT p.* FROM products p JOIN sales s ON p.id = s.product_id GROUP BY p.id HAVING SUM(s.amount) > (SELECT AVG(product_rev) FROM (SELECT SUM(s2.amount) AS product_rev FROM products p2 JOIN sales s2 ON p2.id = s2.product_id GROUP BY p2.id) avg_rev);

sample_query: Show the category with the highest total sales revenue.
sql_query: SELECT c.* FROM categories c JOIN products p ON p.category_id = c.id JOIN sales s ON s.product_id = p.id GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY SUM(s.revenue) DESC LIMIT 1
